what_it_does: An acceptance test script that exercises Suite inbox surfacing to verify node-bound surfaced items persist a ui:// target inside payload, existing consumers still classify and read items unchanged, no-target and node-less items remain unchanged, surface_output attaches a ui://canvas/<node-id> target, and a CONFIRM-tier address click surfaced for approval carries the clicked ui:// address as target while non-ui addresses carry none.
observations: Uses a temporary FsStore and NodeRegistry discovery against the local nodes directory.
Defines a check helper that increments a global PASS counter and asserts conditions.
Defines accepted_by_resolver as a simplified mirror of the frontend resolveUiTarget grammar.
Derives a ui://canvas/<node> target via suite._registry_ui_target and surfaces it inside payload.
Verifies inbox_lanes still classifies the item after the additive payload field.
Surfaces code_build items without ui_target to confirm unchanged behavior.
Creates a constant node, runs its graph, and calls surface_output to inspect the surfaced item payload.
Mutates suite.UI_REGISTRY to inject tiered address entries for the surfaced_for_approval case.
Uses suite.act with a ui:// address and a run:// address to test positive and negative target propagation.
Cleans up the temporary store directory in a try/finally block.
standouts: Tests the backend half of click-to-thing: payload ui_target persistence and resolveUiTarget-accepted grammar.
Demonstrates surface_output attaching ui://canvas/<node-id> as a resolvable camera target.
Includes both positive ui:// address propagation and a negative guard for non-ui addresses.
conventions: Top-level executable test script with inline assertions and a global pass counter.
Direct sys.path insertion and local nodes directory discovery.
Open-dict payload access via .get to tolerate additive fields.
Temporary filesystem store with explicit cleanup.
Heavy use of comments to tie assertions to spec clauses and seams-engine principles.
concerns: Relies on the private/underscore method _registry_ui_target and direct UI_REGISTRY mutation, which may not be stable API.
accepted_by_resolver is a simplified mirror of frontend grammar and could drift from the real resolver.
Hardcodes identifiers like node id 'c' and graph id 'l8-target-graph'.
The comment labels jump from section 5 to section 7, suggesting an editing oversight.
notes: An executable acceptance spec for making surfaced inbox items clickable by carrying a resolveUiTarget-compatible ui:// address in payload.
questions: Does Inbox.surface already preserve arbitrary payload keys, or is this test asserting desired behavior not yet implemented elsewhere?
What is the exact return shape of surface_output across all code paths (dict with 'surfaced', 'id', or raw id)?
Is mutating suite.UI_REGISTRY a supported test seam or a temporary workaround?
Where is the canonical resolveUiTarget grammar and how is accepted_by_resolver kept in sync?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it tests payload ui_target persistence, resolveUiTarget-accepted forms, consumer preservation, and no-target cases for surfaced inbox items.
apparent_themes: Open-dict extensibility without breaking existing consumers
Avoidance of circular or meaningless navigation links
Additive change guarded by grammar discriminator
intention_signals: Encode the L8 acceptance criteria that surfaced inbox items should be directly clickable by carrying a resolveUiTarget-compatible ui:// address in payload, while leaving existing readers and node-less items untouched.
novelty: Combines registry-derived node targets, surface_output demonstrative targets, and CONFIRM-tier surfaced_for_approval address propagation into a single acceptance test with a grammar discriminator.
contribution: A runnable acceptance test that documents and guards the contract that surfaced inbox items may include a ui:// target inside payload, that the target is resolveUiTarget-compatible, that existing consumers continue to work, and that node-less or non-ui cases remain targetless.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test for the company project that verifies surfaced inbox items carry a resolveUiTarget-compatible ui:// target inside their payload, existing consumers remain unaffected, and node-less or non-ui items stay targetless.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an executable spec that backend-surfaced inbox items expose a clickable ui:// navigation target in payload without changing existing readers.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: A top-level test helper that takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL message, increments the PASS counter, and prints an ok line. It drives the script's lightweight assertion style.

symbols.does: Returns whether a target string is accepted by the frontend resolveUiTarget grammar.
symbols.description: A simplified predicate mirroring the frontend resolver: it accepts only strings starting with ui://, rejecting run:// and other forms. It is used as a discriminator to catch stored-but-unnavigable targets.
